sources.author: Swyx
sources.headline: Founder @ Latte Academy | Previously @Expedia @Shipworks | angel investor
sources.post_summary: Swyx discusses the evolution of AI agents, emphasizing multi-agent systems and their potential to automate complex workflows beyond single-task agents. He shares insights from recent demos and predicts widespread adoption in developer tools. Highlights challenges in reliability and orchestration.

sources.author: Andrej Karpathy
sources.headline: Founder @ Eureka Labs | Previously @OpenAI @Tesla
sources.post_summary: Karpathy explores AI infrastructure needs for training and deploying agentic systems at scale, critiquing current cloud dependencies. He advocates for optimized hardware like custom TPUs and shares benchmarks on inference speed for agent loops. Calls for open-source contributions to infra tooling.

sources.author: Guillermo Rauch
sources.headline: CEO @ Vercel
sources.post_summary: Rauch announces new developer tools at Vercel for building AI agents with seamless deployment and edge inference. Details how it integrates automation pipelines for real-time agent interactions. Includes a live demo and invites feedback from the dev community.

sources.author: Nat Friedman
sources.headline: Co-Founder @ GitHub (former)
sources.post_summary: Friedman analyzes the shift to AI-driven automation in software development, focusing on agentic code generation tools. Predicts 10x productivity gains but warns of skill gaps. Shares examples from recent pilots with enterprise teams.

sources.author: Sarah Guo
sources.headline: Founder & General Partner @ Conviction Partners
sources.post_summary: Guo breaks down investment trends in AI infrastructure for agent ecosystems, spotlighting startups in vector DBs and orchestration layers. Discusses how these enable reliable multi-agent automation. Provides a curated list of emerging tools.

sources.author: Sami Rahman
sources.headline: Founder @ Ramp Ventures | GTM Engineer
sources.post_summary: Discusses building scalable GTM engines from scratch, emphasizing the integration of product-led growth with sales motions. Shares a framework for aligning engineering with revenue teams to reduce CAC. Highlights real-world examples from early-stage SaaS companies.

sources.author: Josh Braun
sources.headline: Sales Coach | Author | Keynote Speaker
sources.post_summary: Explains why outbound automation often fails without personalized intent data and human touchpoints. Provides a step-by-step guide to layering automation on top of high-signal prospecting. Warns against common pitfalls like spray-and-pray emailing.

sources.author: Aja Frost
sources.headline: Head of Content Strategy @ HubSpot
sources.post_summary: Outlines a data-driven content strategy for revenue teams, focusing on bottom-of-funnel assets that drive pipeline. Shares metrics from HubSpot's experiments showing 3x better conversion with buyer-stage aligned content. Includes templates for sales ops integration.

sources.author: Dan Khan
sources.headline: RevOps Leader | Ex-Snowflake | Podcast Host
sources.post_summary: Breaks down the RevOps stack for 2024, prioritizing tools for GTM alignment and outbound scaling. Details how to operationalize sales ops with no-code automation to hit ARR goals. Case study on reducing ramp time by 40%.

sources.author: Katelyn Bourgoin
sources.headline: Customer Growth Expert | GTM Advisor
sources.post_summary: Explores content strategy as the backbone of outbound and GTM motions, with frameworks for creating assets that warm leads pre-engagement. Analyzes why sales ops teams undervalue content velocity and shares ROI calculations.

sources.author: Pieter Levels
sources.headline: Indie Maker & Founder of Nomad List, Remote OK
sources.post_summary: Pieter shares his journey as a solo developer shipping multiple products like Nomad List and Remote OK without VC funding. He emphasizes the power of building in public, iterating based on user feedback, and achieving profitability through bootstrapping. His post details the latest features shipped and revenue updates.

sources.author: Arvid Kahl
sources.headline: Serial Entrepreneur, Author of Zero to Sold
sources.post_summary: Arvid recounts pivoting from corporate life to solo founding FeedbackPanda, which he built and sold for 7 figures. He advises on validating ideas quickly and shipping MVPs as a solo dev. The post includes lessons on audience building and exit strategies.

sources.author: KP (Karthik Pattabiraman)
sources.headline: Solo Founder & Builder at LlamaIndex
sources.post_summary: KP details his career pivot from AI research to shipping LlamaIndex, an open-source tool with millions of downloads. He shares the challenges of solo development, public updates on progress, and tips for aspiring builders. The post highlights recent product launches and community growth.

sources.author: Daniel Vassallo
sources.headline: Ex-AWS, Indie Maker & Small Bets Creator
sources.post_summary: Daniel discusses leaving a high-paying tech job to pursue small bets as a solo builder, shipping products like Grow with Daniel. He focuses on the freedom of independent work, public sharing of wins and failures, and financial independence. Readers engage with his pivot story and product updates.

sources.author: Sahil Lavingia
sources.headline: Founder & CEO of Gumroad
sources.post_summary: Sahil reflects on building Gumroad as a solo founder initially, pivoting from a failed VC-backed startup to profitable bootstrapping. He shares shipping philosophy, public metrics, and advice for creators shipping digital products. The post inspires many on tech career shifts.
